mission.title: Install and Configure Flowise
mission.description: Flowise is an AI platform that allows users to build AI agents visually. It is ideal for developers looking to create and manage AI workflows without deep programming knowledge. Key trade-offs include the need for Node.js and Docker for installation and potential resource consumption during operation.

steps.title: Install the Flowise CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Flowise command-line interface globally on your machine.

```bash

npm install -g flowise@3.0.13

```

> Install the specific version of Flowise CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create flowise-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Deploy Flowise on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys Flowise using a Helm chart.

```bash

helm repo add flowise https://github.com/FlowiseAI/Flowise/releases/download/3.0.13

helm install flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise --create-namespace

```

> Add the Flowise Helm repository.
> Install Flowise in its own namespace.

steps.title: Verify Flowise Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Flowise pods to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n flowise

```

> Look for the status of Flowise pods to be 'Running'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling and monitoring for your Flowise deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment flowise --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 -n flowise

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Flowise
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Flowise deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall flowise --namespace flowise

gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> This removes the Flowise release.
> This deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flowise
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This process includes draining the pods, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise

kubectl get pods -n flowise

```

> Replace <pod-name> with the name of your Flowise pod.
> Upgrade to the latest version of Flowise.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the image is not available or the repository is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the application is failing to start properly.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n flowise' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This happens when the cluster does not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the service is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service is correctly configured and the correct port is being used.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new visual components for AI agent workflows, improved performance metrics, and enhanced user interface.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed legacy API endpoints that are no longer supported.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, ensure to migrate your configurations to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the Flowise UI accessible at http://localhost:3000 with all services running smoothly.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
